openmeter/productcatalog/addon.purpose: Domain package for add-on lifecycle management: defines the Addon aggregate (with RateCards), domain types (Plan, RateCard, RateCards), typed errors, domain events, and the Service + Repository interfaces. Primary constraint: domain types must always carry both productcatalog.* base types and managed fields (NamespacedID, ManagedModel).

patterns.name: Addon aggregate embeds productcatalog.AddonMeta + RateCards
patterns.description: Addon struct embeds models.NamespacedID, models.ManagedModel, productcatalog.AddonMeta, and addon.RateCards. RateCard wraps productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields (AddonID field).
patterns.example: type Addon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.AddonMeta; RateCards RateCards }

patterns.name: Typed domain errors wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: NotFoundError wraps models.NewGenericNotFoundError via Unwrap chain. Always use NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}) and IsNotFound() — never return raw Ent not-found errors.
patterns.example: return nil, addon.NewNotFoundError(addon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, ID: id})

patterns.name: Domain events implement metadata.EventName / EventMetadata / Validate
patterns.description: Each lifecycle event (Create, Update, Delete, Publish, Archive) is a struct with Addon *Addon + UserID *string, implementing EventName(), EventMetadata(), and Validate(). EventName uses metadata.GetEventName with subsystem + name + version.
patterns.example: func (e AddonCreateEvent) EventName() string { return metadata.GetEventName(metadata.EventType{Subsystem: AddonEventSubsystem, Name: AddonCreateEventName, Version: "v1"}) }

patterns.name: Input types implement models.Validator; IgnoreNonCriticalIssues for partial validation
patterns.description: CreateAddonInput and UpdateAddonInput embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues bool}. Validate() calls models.AsValidationIssues and filters by ErrorSeverityCritical when set.
patterns.example: issues, err := models.AsValidationIssues(errors.Join(errs...)); if i.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues { issues = issues.WithSeverityOrHigher(models.ErrorSeverityCritical) }

patterns.name: RateCards uses AsProductCatalogRateCards() for cross-type conversion
patterns.description: addon.RateCards.AsProductCatalogRateCards() returns productcatalog.RateCards; used in assert helpers and Addon.AsProductCatalogAddon(). Never directly cast the slice.
patterns.example: func (c RateCards) AsProductCatalogRateCards() productcatalog.RateCards { ... }

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[Addon] pattern for status/deletion guards
patterns.description: validators.go exposes IsAddonDeleted and HasAddonStatus as models.ValidatorFunc[Addon] closures, composed via addon.Validate() via ValidateWith.
patterns.example: func HasAddonStatus(statuses ...productcatalog.AddonStatus) models.ValidatorFunc[Addon] { return func(a Addon) error { ... } }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Service interface (ListAddons, CreateAddon, DeleteAddon, GetAddon, UpdateAddon, PublishAddon, ArchiveAddon, NextAddon), all input types, and their Validate() methods.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishAddonInput.Validate() enforces EffectiveFrom not in the past with a 30s jitter; clock.Now() is used — do not substitute time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Addon aggregate type with Validate(), ValidateWith(), and AsProductCatalogAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plans field is *[]Plan — optional; only populated on expand.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wraps productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields; custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON dispatches on productcatalog.RateCardSerde.Type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UnmarshalJSON must switch on FlatFeeRateCardType vs UsageBasedRateCardType — any new type needs a case here.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError type wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError; IsNotFound() for errors.As detection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never return raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap via NewNotFoundError.

key_file_guides.role: All five lifecycle event structs; UserID sourced from session.GetSessionUserID(ctx).
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddonDeleteEvent.Validate() asserts Addon.DeletedAt != nil; ensure soft-delete is applied before publishing.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface extends entutils.TxCreator — required for TransactingRepo in adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TxCreator embedding is mandatory; adapters without it cannot participate in ctx-bound transactions.

key_file_guides.role: IsAddonDeleted and HasAddonStatus validator funcs for use in service-layer guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use these funcs via Addon.ValidateWith() instead of inline status checks.

anti_patterns: Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound errors — always wrap in addon.NewNotFoundError.
Using context.Background() in service or adapter code — always propagate ctx.
Calling RateCards adapter operations without eager-loading related rate cards (WithRatecards).
Publishing domain events outside a transaction closure — event failure should roll back the DB write.
Creating multiple non-deleted draft versions for the same key without checking HasDraft().

decisions.decision: RateCard is a separate managed type wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with AddonID
decisions.rationale: Each rate card needs its own DB identity (NamespacedID) and foreign-key reference (AddonID) separate from the base productcatalog.RateCard so Ent can persist and eager-load them independently.

decisions.decision: inputOptions.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues enables partial validation for draft addons
decisions.rationale: Draft addons can be created with non-critical issues (e.g. missing features not yet registered), so callers can opt into a relaxed validation that only fails on ErrorSeverityCritical.

decisions.decision: Domain events carry the full Addon pointer + UserID from session context
decisions.rationale: Downstream Watermill consumers need the full entity for side-effects; UserID supports audit trails without requiring a separate lookup.

code_examples.scenario: Creating and publishing a domain event after a successful adapter write

openmeter/productcatalog/plan.purpose: Domain package for plan lifecycle management: defines Plan (with Phases and RateCards), Phase/RateCard managed types, typed errors, domain events, a custom JSON serializer, and the Service + Repository interfaces. Primary constraint: EffectivePeriod changes are only allowed via Publish/Archive, never via UpdatePlan.

patterns.name: Plan embeds PlanMeta + []Phase; Phase embeds PhaseManagedFields + productcatalog.Phase
patterns.description: Plan struct = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanMeta + []Phase. Phase = PhaseManagedFields{ManagedModel, NamespacedID, PlanID} + productcatalog.Phase. RateCard = productcatalog.RateCard + RateCardManagedFields{PhaseID}.
patterns.example: type Plan struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanMeta; Phases []Phase }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: Same pattern as addon: NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}), IsNotFound() for errors.As. Never surface raw Ent not-found.
patterns.example: return nil, plan.NewNotFoundError(plan.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, Key: key, Version: v})

patterns.name: Custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON on Plan for polymorphic RateCards
patterns.description: serializer.go implements Plan.MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON using alias types to avoid recursion. RateCards serialized with RateCardSerde type discriminator (flat_fee / usage_based).
patterns.example: json.Marshal(plan) // uses Plan.MarshalJSON which dispatches through rateCardAlias

patterns.name: EffectivePeriod zeroed in UpdatePlanInput to prevent direct status manipulation
patterns.description: UpdatePlanInput embeds productcatalog.EffectivePeriod but service must zero it before calling adapter. Callers cannot set status via update; only Publish/Archive are valid paths.
patterns.example: // service: input.EffectivePeriod = productcatalog.EffectivePeriod{} before calling adapter.UpdatePlan

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[Plan] for status/deletion guards
patterns.description: validators.go provides IsPlanDeleted(at) and HasPlanStatus(statuses...) as composable models.ValidatorFunc[Plan] used with Plan.ValidateWith().
patterns.example: if err := p.ValidateWith(plan.HasPlanStatus(productcatalog.PlanStatusDraft)); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Input types embed inputOptions and call models.AsValidationIssues
patterns.description: CreatePlanInput and UpdatePlanInput both embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues} and use models.AsValidationIssues+WithSeverityOrHigher in Validate().
patterns.example: issues, err := models.AsValidationIssues(errors.Join(errs...)); if i.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues { issues = issues.WithSeverityOrHigher(models.ErrorSeverityCritical) }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (ListPlans, CreatePlan, DeletePlan, GetPlan, UpdatePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan, NextPlan) and all input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishPlanInput.Validate() uses a 30s jitter clock check for EffectiveFrom; use pkg/clock.Now() not time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Plan aggregate with Validate(), ValidateWith(), AsProductCatalogPlan().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Addons field is *[]Addon — only populated on expand. Never assume it is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Phase and PhaseManagedFields types; ValidatePhaseManagedFields() and ValidatePhase() validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PhaseManagedFields.Validate() asserts PlanID != '' — always populate PlanID when constructing a Phase in adapters.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with PhaseID; custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON dispatches on type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rate card types require a case in UnmarshalJSON and the serializer switch.

key_file_guides.role: Custom JSON marshaler/unmarshaler for Plan; uses alias types to avoid recursion, rateCardAlias for polymorphic cards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: serializer_test.go covers round-trip plus error paths; any new field on Plan/Phase/RateCard must be reflected here.

key_file_guides.role: IsPlanDeleted and HasPlanStatus validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsPlanDeleted takes a time.Time argument (the check time); use clock.Now() at the call site.

anti_patterns: Setting EffectivePeriod via UpdatePlan — it must be zeroed in the service; only Publish/Archive alter it.
Skipping resolveFeatures/resolveTaxCodes before persisting rate cards in the service.
Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap in plan.NewNotFoundError.
Publishing events outside a transaction.Run closure — DB write and event publish must be atomic.
Editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files — always regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: EffectivePeriod changes are strictly gated to Publish/Archive operations
decisions.rationale: UpdatePlan explicitly zeroes EffectivePeriod to prevent callers from manipulating plan status through the update path; status transitions are explicit state-machine operations.

decisions.decision: Custom JSON serializer on Plan instead of generated oapi-codegen types
decisions.rationale: productcatalog.RateCard is a polymorphic interface; the serializer dispatches on the type discriminator to pick the correct concrete struct, which generated decoders cannot handle.

decisions.decision: Version auto-incremented in service layer, not settable by callers
decisions.rationale: Prevents callers from forging version numbers; the service computes the next version from existing versions at create/next time.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a service mutation with transaction and event publish

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon.purpose: Domain package for plan-to-addon assignment (PlanAddon): defines the PlanAddon aggregate, typed errors, domain events, and the Service + Repository interfaces. PlanAddon links a plan.Plan and an addon.Addon with quantity constraints and phase anchoring.

patterns.name: PlanAddon embeds productcatalog.PlanAddonMeta + plan.Plan + addon.Addon
patterns.description: PlanAddon = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanAddonMeta + Plan plan.Plan + Addon addon.Addon. AsProductCatalogPlanAddon() converts to the base productcatalog type.
patterns.example: type PlanAddon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanAddonMeta; Plan plan.Plan; Addon addon.Addon }

patterns.name: NotFoundError with PlanIDOrKey + AddonIDOrKey params
patterns.description: NotFoundErrorParams supports namespace, ID, PlanIDOrKey, and AddonIDOrKey fields; IsNotFound() detects via errors.As.
patterns.example: return nil, planaddon.NewNotFoundError(planaddon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, PlanIDOrKey: planKey, AddonIDOrKey: addonKey})

patterns.name: Domain events carry PlanAddon pointer + UserID from session
patterns.description: Three events: PlanAddonCreateEvent, PlanAddonUpdateEvent, PlanAddonDeleteEvent. Delete event Validate() asserts PlanAddon.DeletedAt != nil.
patterns.example: event := planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent(ctx, created); s.publisher.Publish(ctx, eventbus.SystemTopic, event)

patterns.name: UpdatePlanAddonInput.Equal(PlanAddon) for idempotency detection
patterns.description: UpdatePlanAddonInput implements models.Equaler[PlanAddon]; service can check if the patch produces any change before calling adapter.
patterns.example: if input.Equal(*existing) { return existing, nil } // skip adapter call

patterns.name: GetPlanAddonInput supports lookup by assignment ID or plan+addon pair
patterns.description: If ID is empty, PlanIDOrKey and AddonIDOrKey must both be provided. Same dual-lookup pattern as plan.GetPlanInput.
patterns.example: if i.ID == '' { if i.PlanIDOrKey == '' || i.AddonIDOrKey == '' { return err } }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (ListPlanAddons, CreatePlanAddon, DeletePlanAddon, GetPlanAddon, UpdatePlanAddon) and all input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreatePlanAddonInput requires PlanID and AddonID non-empty; ID-only lookup and plan+addon-pair lookup are both valid in Get/Update/Delete inputs.

key_file_guides.role: PlanAddon aggregate with Validate() and AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() calls both Plan.Validate() and Addon.Validate(); ensure Plan and Addon are fully populated before constructing.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError and IsNotFound; wraps models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Error message format is 'plan add-on assignment not found [namespace=... plan.idOrKey=... addon.idOrKey=...]'.

key_file_guides.role: Three lifecycle events; subsystem is 'planaddon'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PlanAddonDeleteEvent.Validate() requires PlanAddon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must precede event construction.

key_file_guides.role: Repository extends entutils.TxCreator; 5-method CRUD interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TxCreator is required for TransactingRepo in adapter layer.

key_file_guides.role: Test helpers AssertPlanAddonCreateInputEqual, AssertPlanAddonUpdateInputEqual, AssertPlanAddonEqual.
key_file_guides.watch_for: These are in the planaddon package (not a separate testutils sub-package) so they are importable in adapter/service tests.

anti_patterns: Checking plan/addon status (draft, active) inside the adapter — status validation belongs in the service layer.
Returning raw adapter-level errors without wrapping in planaddon.NewNotFoundError.
Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run for write paths — risks partial writes if event publish fails.
Hard-deleting PlanAddon rows instead of soft-deleting via SetDeletedAt.
Skipping eager-load of Plan and Addon edges after create/update — PlanAddon.Plan and PlanAddon.Addon must always be fully populated.

decisions.decision: PlanAddon carries full plan.Plan and addon.Addon embedded rather than just IDs
decisions.rationale: Callers (subscription sync, billing) need the full plan and addon data without additional lookups; eager-loading at the adapter layer avoids N+1 queries.

decisions.decision: Both ID-based and plan+addon pair-based lookups supported in Get/Update/Delete inputs
decisions.rationale: HTTP handlers and internal code may have either the assignment ID or only the plan/addon identifiers; supporting both avoids forcing a separate Get before Update.

openmeter/productcatalog/subscription.purpose: Bridges the productcatalog plan model to the subscription domain: defines PlanSubscriptionService (Create, Migrate, Change), PlanInput (ref vs inline discriminator), and adapter types (Plan, Phase, RateCard) that implement the subscription.Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces. Primary constraint: all subscription DB writes go through subscriptionworkflow.Service, not directly through subscription.Service.

patterns.name: PlanInput dual-path: ref vs inline plan.CreatePlanInput
patterns.description: PlanInput holds either a *PlanRefInput (existing plan lookup) or *plan.CreatePlanInput (inline spec). Use FromRef/FromInput setters; AsRef()/AsInput() return nil for the unused path.
patterns.example: var pi plansubscription.PlanInput; pi.FromRef(&PlanRefInput{Key: 'pro', Version: lo.ToPtr(2)})

patterns.name: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implement subscription.Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces
patterns.description: plan.go defines Plan, Phase, RateCard wrappers that implement ToCreateSubscriptionPlanInput, ToCreateSubscriptionPhasePlanInput, ToCreateSubscriptionItemPlanInput.
patterns.example: p := &plansubscription.Plan{Plan: fetchedPlan.AsProductCatalogPlan(), Ref: &models.NamespacedID{ID: fetchedPlan.ID}}

patterns.name: PlanSubscriptionService delegates persistence to WorkflowService
patterns.description: Service layer resolves and validates the plan, then calls subscriptionworkflow.Service.CreateFromPlan / ChangeToPlan / Migrate. No direct subscription.Repository calls.
patterns.example: return s.workflowService.CreateFromPlan(ctx, workflowInput, &adaptedPlan)

patterns.name: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase collapses earlier phases in place
patterns.description: When StartingPhase is set, all phases before the starting phase have their Duration zeroed (not deleted) so the subscription begins at the correct phase.
patterns.example: // service/create.go: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase(plan, *request.StartingPhase)

key_file_guides.role: PlanSubscriptionService interface with Create, Migrate, Change; request types MigrateSubscriptionRequest, ChangeSubscriptionRequest, CreateSubscriptionRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChangeSubscriptionRequest.PlanInput and StartingPhase must both be set consistently; StartingPhase is only used when PlanInput.AsRef() is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types that wrap productcatalog types and implement subscription.Plan interfaces; GetPhases() computes cumulative StartAfter durations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plan.GetPhases() computes StartAfter by accumulating phase durations in order — any reordering of Phases breaks timing.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service directly for create/change/migrate — always route through PlanSubscriptionService or WorkflowService.
Calling plan.Service.GetPlan directly instead of the internal getPlanByVersion helper — the helper normalises NotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError.
Setting StartingPhase without calling zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase — phases before the start will not be collapsed.
Importing app/common from testutils — it must stay independent to avoid import cycles.
Adding persistence calls directly in service/service.go — all DB writes go through WorkflowService.

decisions.decision: PlanInput discriminator (ref vs inline) instead of separate Create methods
decisions.rationale: Callers need to subscribe to either an existing stored plan or a transient inline spec without the service needing separate entry points; the discriminator makes the choice explicit.

decisions.decision: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implement subscription interfaces rather than using productcatalog types directly
decisions.rationale: Subscription domain defines its own Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces; the adapter types bridge productcatalog.Plan to those interfaces without circular imports.
